name: Allen Hospital Personnel
description_short: Allen Hospital Personnel Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving the Waterloo, IA community with checking, savings, auto loans, and financial planning services.
description_long:
Allen Hospital Personnel Credit Union operates as a community-focused financial institution based in Waterloo, Iowa. The credit union serves hospital personnel and members of the Waterloo area with a full suite of retail banking services. According to NCUA data referenced on their profile, the credit union maintains 1,154 active share savings accounts, 386 checking accounts, and serves over 240 auto loan members.

The credit union offers high-yield checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balance requirements, flexible auto loans for new and used vehicles, online and mobile banking, financial counseling, and investment/retirement planning services. They provide no-cost share drafts with checking accounts and competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.99% for new vehicles and 7.50% for used vehicles. The credit union emphasizes member benefits including exclusive discounts and dedicated financial planning services.

Allen Hospital Personnel Credit Union distinguishes itself through its nonprofit, member-owned structure typical of credit unions, which allows it to offer competitive rates and return profits to members rather than shareholders. The institution provides personalized financial counseling and maintains lower account minimums on share certificates compared to traditional banks. Their focus on the local Waterloo community and hospital personnel membership base creates a specialized niche.

The profile is limited by minimal transparency regarding total assets, membership size, loan approval rates, or fee structures beyond free checking. The website lacks detailed information about membership eligibility requirements, specific CD rates, or comprehensive fee schedules. As a smaller regional credit union, membership may be restricted to hospital employees or specific community affiliations, which is not explicitly clarified.
services: Regular share savings accounts (1,154 active accounts per NCUA)
Share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balance requirements (24 active per NCUA)
Share draft checking accounts (386 active accounts per NCUA)
Free checking accounts with no monthly fees and free checks
New auto loans with rates starting at 3.99%
Used auto loans with rates starting at 7.50%
Online banking and bill pay services
Financial counseling and personalized financial planning
ATM access and debit card services
pros: NCUA-insured deposits with member-owned nonprofit structure returning profits to members
Competitive auto loan rates: 3.99% for new vehicles, 7.50% for used vehicles (per NCUA data)
No-cost share drafts and free checking accounts with no monthly fees
Share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balance requirements
Free financial counseling services included for members
Robust online banking and mobile banking capabilities with secure loan payment system
Average auto loan of $17,000 with rates available to over 240 members
cons: Unclear membership eligibility requirements—may be limited to hospital personnel or specific groups
Limited branch/ATM network as a small regional credit union
Website does not disclose specific CD rates, savings account APYs, or comprehensive fee schedule
No information on business lending services or commercial account options
Share certificate account volume is very low (24 active CDs), suggesting limited product sophistication
best_for: Allen Hospital employees seeking dedicated financial services and competitive auto lending
Waterloo, IA residents wanting community-focused banking with personalized financial counseling
Auto buyers seeking competitive rates on new and used vehicle financing
Members prioritizing member-owned financial institutions with nonprofit structure
diagnosis: Allen Hospital Personnel Credit Union is best suited for Waterloo-area residents and hospital personnel seeking competitive auto financing and community-focused banking with personal service. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility may be restricted to hospital employees or specific community affiliations, and the credit union does not publicly disclose membership requirements, making it essential to verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
